title: Add levels and sword weapons to the 3D fighting game

requirements.summary: Implement multi-level progression system with at least 3 distinct levels, increasing difficulty, level-transition screens, victory screen, and HUD display of current level number
acceptanceCriteria: The game has at least 3 playable levels with increasing difficulty.
Defeating all enemies in a level advances the player to the next level.
A level-transition screen is shown between levels, displaying the current level number.
Completing all levels shows a final victory screen.
The current level number is shown in the HUD alongside health bars.
Enemy count, speed, or strength scales with each level.

file_operations.description: Add currentLevel number field to GameState interface to track the active level. Add levelDifficulty configuration types defining enemy count, speed multiplier, and strength for each level.

Add level progression logic that initializes currentLevel at 1, tracks enemies defeated per level, and advances to the next level when all enemies are cleared. Configure enemy spawn parameters (count, speed, strength) based on currentLevel using difficulty scaling rules. Trigger level-transition and victory screens at appropriate times. Integrate the LevelTransitionScreen and VictoryScreen components into the render tree with conditional visibility based on game state.

Create a React component that displays a full-screen overlay showing the current level number during level transitions. Use a fade-in/fade-out animation and auto-dismiss after 2-3 seconds to resume gameplay. Style with theme-consistent colors and typography from index.css.

Create a React component that displays a full-screen victory overlay when all levels are completed. Show congratulatory text, final stats (if available), and a replay or return-to-menu button. Style with emissive glow effects and fighting-game theme colors.

Create a React component that renders the in-game HUD overlay, displaying the current level number alongside existing health bars for UG and enemies. Position the level indicator in the top-center or top-left of the screen using absolute positioning. Use consistent typography and color scheme from index.css.

Integrate the GameHUD component into the scene render tree so the level number is visible during gameplay. Ensure the HUD overlay renders above the 3D canvas without obscuring critical gameplay elements.

Add support for per-enemy speed and strength multipliers based on the current level difficulty. Accept level-scaled parameters (moveSpeed, attackDamage) and apply them during state transitions and attack calculations. Ensure backward compatibility with existing AI behavior.

requirements.summary: Add sword weapon for UG with dedicated keyboard key (E) and on-screen SWORD button, rendering a 3D sword mesh in UG's hand, increasing melee range and damage, and displaying a sword slash visual effect during attacks
acceptanceCriteria: UG can activate a sword attack using a dedicated keyboard key (e.g., 'E').
A 3D sword mesh is rendered in UG's hand when the sword attack is triggered.
The sword attack deals more damage and/or has greater range than default melee attacks.
A visible sword slash arc or sweep emissive effect appears during the attack animation.
A SWORD button is added to the OnScreenControls overlay and triggers the sword attack on tap/click with mousedown/mouseup and touchstart/touchend support.
The sword attack integrates with the existing combat and hit-detection systems, causing Sukuna to lose health on a successful sword hit.

file_operations.description: Add 'sword' boolean field to KeyState interface to track sword attack input. Add swordActive boolean and swordDamageMultiplier number fields to the Character interface to represent sword weapon state.

Extend keyboard event listeners to track the 'E' key state and map it to the 'sword' field in the returned KeyState ref. Ensure the sword key is handled with the same press/release pattern as existing action keys.

Add a SWORD button to the on-screen controls overlay following the same mousedown/mouseup and touchstart/touchend event pattern as existing buttons (punch, kick, gun). Position the button in a logical location within the controls layout. Use an appropriate SVG icon or label for the sword action. Update the KeyState ref when the button is pressed/released.

Add a 3D sword mesh (e.g., a long thin box or cylinder with an emissive blade material) as a child of UG's hand or arm mesh. The sword should only be visible when the character's swordActive flag is true. Include a SwordSlashEffect component that renders an arc or sweep emissive geometry during the sword attack animation, positioned relative to the sword mesh. Use React Three Fiber's useFrame or animation state to trigger the slash visual when the sword attack is active.

Add logic to handle the sword key state from KeyState ref. When the sword key is pressed, set the player character's swordActive flag to true and trigger a sword attack animation. Pass the swordActive state and swordDamageMultiplier to the combat system for hit detection and damage calculation. Ensure the sword attack respects existing attack cooldowns and animation timing.

Extend melee hit detection logic to check if the player's swordActive flag is true. If a sword attack is active, increase the attack hitbox range and apply the swordDamageMultiplier to the base damage. Ensure the sword attack triggers the same hit particles, knockback, and damage flash effects as existing melee attacks. Maintain compatibility with the existing combatSystem interface.

Update the attack hitbox calculation utility to accept an optional swordActive flag. When the sword is active, extend the hitbox range (e.g., 1.5x or 2x the default melee range) to reflect the sword's longer reach. Ensure the extended hitbox integrates cleanly with the existing rectangle overlap detection.

requirements.summary: Extend TypeScript type definitions in game.ts to include 'sword' boolean in KeyState, 'currentLevel' number in GameState, and sword-attack state fields (swordActive, swordDamageMultiplier) in Character interface
acceptanceCriteria: GameState or equivalent top-level state includes a 'currentLevel' number field.
Character interface includes a swordActive flag or equivalent sword-attack state.
All existing usages of KeyState and Character remain type-safe after the additions.

file_operations.description: Add 'sword: boolean' field to the KeyState interface. Add 'currentLevel: number' field to the GameState interface. Add 'swordActive: boolean' and 'swordDamageMultiplier: number' fields to the Character interface. Ensure all new fields are properly typed and integrate cleanly with existing type definitions without breaking type-safety in consuming code.
